### 06.06.2025

María, Anniina

- **Omentum gel (10 min)**

> Is DNAse inactivated/degraded at -80, or in time?
>
> Ask for DNAse treatment protocol (amount, time, etc), and the qPCR
> results for DNA treatment, or how else did they check the DNA%?

- **scRNA-seq planning: experimental plan & practical arrangements
 > with Heidi's people (40 min)**

### 02.05.2025

María, Anniina

- **Recap (5 min)**

- **Plan scRNA-seq meeting with Heidi Haikala's lab (35 min)**

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Tentative list of topics to discuss:

- First briefly a recap about the model: cell types + possible drugs
 > (What questions do we want to address with the model: e.g. are the
 > phenotypes changing when +/- fibroblasts and/or drugs). Should we
 > do a pilot now? Eg with and without fibroblasts. What are the
 > steps in which we need to start putting money -\> Doing
 > experiments without actually sequencing.

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Minimum cell requirement

- Minimum viability requirement:

> According to my discussion with Jun, washes can help slightly improve
> viability, but only marginally. He once tried a dead cell removal kit
> many years ago, but they didn't really evaluate the improvement.
> However, he mentioned that since many people are now using and
> recommending this step, it might be a good idea to include it.
>

ut they didn't really evaluate the improvement.
> However, he mentioned that since many people are now using and
> recommending this step, it might be a good idea to include it.
>
> Another way to compensate for low viability is to use more cells per
> condition, although the chip has an upper input limit.
>
> Mitochondrial percentage (% mito) is commonly used as a QC metric
> after sequencing. However, Jun believes the best strategy is to aim
> for the most viable preps possible.

- Can samples be processed in batches, or is it better to process them
 > individually? Can we for instance combine PATIENTS in the library
 > prep step. SNP array separation of the patients. This might be the
 > most cost efficient but we are not sure if this is possible. Maybe
 > do pilot +/- fibroblasts for maybe 3 patients and then do drug
 > testing (maybe multiplexing patients with SNP arrays)

- Suggestions for UMI-tagged antibodies?

- How many conditions can we multiplex per patient?

- Costs / logistics / location / other considerations...???

```{=html}

```
- **Aino's contract (15 min)**

> Talk to Aino and explain the possibility to continue in a shared-basis
> agreement with Anna V. It might be good for Aino to contact Anna ASAP.

### 08.04.2025

María, Anniina

nna V. It might be good for Aino to contact Anna ASAP.

### 08.04.2025

María, Anniina

- **Recap (5 min)**

- **Quick update on the project: results of last sample\'s experiments
 > and implications for future scRNA seq and additional readouts (15
 > min)**

> ***- Cell type composition tracking in the cultures:***
>
> Two additional samples (for a total of three samples) with the aim of
> characterizing the cultures and showing that the cells are present for
> the subsequent readouts, not necessarily that the proportions don't
> change over time. For this, continue doing flow cytometry at d0, d3,
> d6, and d9.
>
> ***- Timeline for different readouts:***
>
> This should be determined later on based on the mechanism of action of
> the selected drugs.
>
> ***- Viability over culturing:***
>
> Before doing anything on the experimental side, I will discuss the
> viability of the cultures for the scRNA seq and subsequent analysis
> with 1) Heidi and her people, and 2) Jun Dai. Anniina will also ask if
> Anna has insights on this.

- **Next steps: considering IF imaging of the cultures with confocal
 > microscopy at FIMM BIU for characterization of the cultures (10
 > min)**

> Training can be arranged, and the feasibility of obtaining
> high-quality images can be explored. However, this is not a top
> priority.
